name: Sierra de las Estancias - Santuario del Saliente Loop
name_original: De Vélez-Rubio hasta Albox — circular por las Ramblas de la Sierra de las Estancias
description: This difficult 78.6-mile road cycling route from Vélez-Rubio to Albox traverses the Sierra de las Estancias with 7259 feet of climbing.
region_intro: Road cycling around Vélez-Rubio offers diverse terrains within the province of Almería, Andalusia. The region features a mix of landscapes, including the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park with its pine and holm oak forests. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse valleys, ascend mountain passes, and wind through a Mediterranean landscape. The area provides both smooth asphalt sections and climbs with significant elevation gains, catering to various fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Vélez-Rubio The most popular road cycling route is Las dos Velez: Velez-Blanco y Velez-Rubio — circular by the Sierra Maria Natural Park, a 28.1 miles (45.2 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route circulates through the Sierra María Natural Park, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de las Estancias - Santuario del Saliente Loop, a difficult 78.6 miles (126.4 km) path. This challenging route features substantial elevation gain through the Sierra de las Estancias. Local road cyclists also love the Vélez-Rubio – Vélez-Blanco loop from Vélez-Rubio, a 26.1 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Vélez-Rubio is defined by its natural parks, pine and holm oak forests, and significant mountain climbs. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Vélez-Rubio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Vélez-Rubio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Vélez-Rubio?
A: Road cycling around Vélez-Rubio offers a varied landscape, from smooth asphalt sections suitable for high-speed riding to challenging climbs. You'll encounter routes winding through valleys, ascending mountain passes, and traversing a Mediterranean landscape, including pine and holm oak forests within the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park.

Q: Are there many challenging road cycling routes in the Vélez-Rubio area?
A: Yes, Vélez-Rubio is known for its challenging routes. Out of over 30 available routes, 19 are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987356119/sierra-de-las-estancias-santuario-del-saliente-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de las Estancias - Santuario del Saliente Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 126.4 km path with over 2,200 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while cycling in Vélez-Rubio?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle through the stunning Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park, known for its forests. Routes often pass by picturesque villages and offer views of surrounding mountains. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2784897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caños de Caravaca Fountain&lt;/a&gt; and the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Blanco Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sierra de María&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Vélez-Rubio?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987646957/las-dos-velez-velez-blanco-y-velez-rubio-circular-by-the-sierra-maria-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las dos Velez: Velez-Blanco y Velez-Rubio — circular by the Sierra Maria Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 45.2 km and circulates through the Sierra María Natural Park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27627759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Rubio – Vélez-Blanco loop from Vélez-Rubio&lt;/a&gt;, a 42.1 km trail.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Vélez-Rubio?
A: The road cycling routes in Vélez-Rubio are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful natural parks, and the challenging mountain climbs that define the area's cycling experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Vélez-Rubio?
A: Vélez-Rubio, located in Andalusia, benefits from a Mediterranean climate. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference for heat, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak summer heat and potential winter chill at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987356119/sierra-de-las-estancias-santuario-del-saliente-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de las Estancias - Santuario del Saliente Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 126.4 km route with substantial elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38218371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera de la Parroquia – Nogalte Castle loop from Vélez-Rubio&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 100 km.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay along the cycling routes in Vélez-Rubio?
A: Yes, the region features picturesque villages and the urban center of Vélez-Rubio itself, which offer amenities. As you cycle through or near towns like Vélez-Blanco and Vélez-Rubio, you'll find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation to refuel and rest.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Vélez-Rubio?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, there are moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987646957/las-dos-velez-velez-blanco-y-velez-rubio-circular-by-the-sierra-maria-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las dos Velez: Velez-Blanco y Velez-Rubio — circular by the Sierra Maria Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 45.2 km route that offers a good balance of challenge and scenic beauty without being overly demanding for those with some cycling experience.

Q: What historical sites can I visit while road cycling in the Vélez-Rubio area?
A: The region is rich in history. You can pass through or visit Vélez-Blanco, home to a majestic castle and the Caños de Caravaca Fountain. Vélez-Rubio itself boasts an interesting architectural complex from the 18th and 19th centuries, including the Church of Our Lady of the Incarnation and the Miguel Guirao Archaeological Museum. The surrounding mountains also feature ancient Moorish fortresses.

Q: Is it possible to combine road cycling with visiting religious sites?
A: Yes, some routes can lead you to significant religious sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987356119/sierra-de-las-estancias-santuario-del-saliente-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de las Estancias - Santuario del Saliente Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2580445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente&lt;/a&gt;, a notable religious building in the area.

Q: How can I find parking for road cycling routes in Vélez-Rubio?
A: Vélez-Rubio, as a town, offers various parking options. Many routes start directly from the town center or nearby, making it convenient to park your vehicle and begin your ride. Look for designated parking areas within the town or at the start points of specific routes.
tips: This sumptuous church was built in the last third of the 17th century on an old sanctuary to house the image of the Virgen del Saliente. According to Uclés and Sintas, this sanctuary is located where the inn is now.

Both the name, al-Boj, and its foundation are of Arabic origin, although the area's habitation dates back to the Neolithic period.

The Phoenician presence was evident in the search for minerals, particularly silver. Later, the Roman and Islamic presence is evident in the abundance of archaeological remains found in the area.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route, rated as 'difficult'. It demands very good fitness due to its substantial distance of 126 km and a considerable elevation gain of 2,210 meters. Cyclists should be prepared for a demanding ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, it's important to note that some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride. The route traverses 'ramblas,' which are typically dry riverbeds and often unpaved. This means a standard road bike might struggle in certain sections, and a more robust road bike or gravel bike could be more suitable.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated completion time for this 126 km route is around 6 hours and 58 minutes, though this can vary based on individual fitness levels and stops.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque and diverse landscapes of the Almería province, including mountains and valleys within the Sierra de las Estancias. You'll pass through settlements like Albox and Taberno, and near the Santuario del Saliente. The region is known for its wild nature and contrasts, with potential views of whitewashed villages.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route connecting Vélez-Rubio and Albox, you can typically find parking in either town to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually available within the town centers or near the main access roads.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be particularly beautiful in late February to early March when almond trees are in bloom, adding vibrant color to the landscape. Generally, spring and autumn offer more pleasant temperatures for cycling in this region compared to the heat of summer.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Sierra de las Estancias?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling this route through the Sierra de las Estancias. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in the natural areas, it's not typically considered 'dog-friendly' for accompanying on a bicycle due to the distance, terrain, and potential traffic. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured and consider their endurance for such a long ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects towns like Vélez-Rubio and Albox, you will find various amenities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in these settlements. It's advisable to plan your stops, especially for water and snacks, as services may be sparse between towns.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths, including sections of the Cerro Alto, Sendero del Picacho, Paseo Av. Adolfo Suarez, Arroyo de Olías o del Villar, and Rambla del Medio.

faqs.question: What type of bicycle is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given that some segments of this route may be unpaved and difficult to ride, a standard road bike might struggle. For a more comfortable and suitable experience, a robust road bike or even a gravel bike is recommended to handle the varied terrain, especially the 'rambla' sections.
